Go example 0.
Goal: teach how to parse input with a loop.
The example uses a slice and a []int value.
```go
func processGo00(items []int) int {
    total := 0
    for _, value := range items {
        if value >= 0 { total += value }
    }
    return total
}
```
// Read the code from top to bottom.
The function receives data, skips invalid values, and returns one clear result.
A common mistake in this topic is nil pointer.
Check the empty input case before trusting the code.
Keep names descriptive, keep steps small, and test one behavior at a time.

Go example 49.
Goal: teach how to validate data with a unit test.
The example uses a slice and a []int value.
```go
func processGo049(items []int) int {
    total := 0
    for _, value := range items {
        if value >= 0 { total += value }

    return total
```

```go
    return total
}
```

A common mistake in this topic is nil pointer.

The example uses a goroutine and a []int value.
```go
func processGo01804(items []int) int {
    total := 0
    for _, value := range items {
        if value >= 0 { total += value }
    }
    return total
}
```
